headline: Typhoon Bavi Empties Three Coastlines
deck: Bavi forced mass evacuations along eastern China, shut down much of Taiwan, and battered Japan's southern islands as it tracked toward the Zhejiang coast. The same storm idled the island that makes most of the world's advanced chips.

art.title: Bavi: Ryukyus to the Zhejiang Coast
art.caption: Typhoon Bavi tracked across the Ryukyus and past Taiwan toward Wenzhou on China's Zhejiang-Fujian coast, a three-jurisdiction coastline event · Map: Sprockett, Escalation Desk · Terrain: NOAA ETOPO1

body: Typhoon Bavi turned a stretch of the western Pacific into a single emergency. Authorities ordered very large evacuations along eastern China, shut down much of Taiwan, and braced Japan's southern islands, with deadly indirect flooding reported in the Philippines from the storm's enhanced monsoon, according to wire reporting on the system [E1]. Forecasters tracked the center toward Wenzhou and Wenling on the Zhejiang-Fujian coast [E1].

The warnings came from more than one government at once. Taiwan's Central Weather Administration and Japan's Meteorological Agency both ran active advisories as the storm crossed the Ryukyus and skirted the island, closing offices, halting transport and grounding flights across a three-jurisdiction coastline [E2]. What makes Bavi a top-tier international story is its scale and trajectory rather than any single warning: a population-scale event spanning China, Taiwan and Japan in the same day [E1].

One of those jurisdictions is the world's chip bench. Taiwan's shutdowns idle an island that fabricates most of the planet's advanced semiconductors, and even a day of paused fabs and blocked ports ripples outward through the same AI-hardware supply chain that a record memory listing was celebrating on the other side of the ocean this week [E1]. The storm is a reminder that compute has a coastline, and the coastline has weather.

The damage is still being counted. Evacuations and shutdowns are precautionary as much as reactive, and the sharpest harm so far is the indirect kind, the monsoon flooding away from the storm's eye rather than the landfall itself [E1]. The discriminators are ordinary and near-term: where Bavi comes ashore hardest, how long Taiwan's fabs and ports stay dark, and whether the Philippine flood toll climbs after the rain [E1][E2].

evidence_box.fragment: Thousands evacuated as Taiwan shuts down for Typhoon Bavi; large evacuations in eastern China and disruption in Japan's southern islands; storm tracking toward the Zhejiang coast
Active tropical-cyclone advisories for Typhoon Bavi across the Ryukyus and southern Japan
